statutoryBodies.name: 1990 Suwaseriya Foundation
statutoryBodies.statusNote: Established by Section 2 as a body corporate with perpetual succession and a common seal. Provides free pre-hospital care ambulance services and emergency response services island-wide. Board of Management comprises 7 members: 4 appointed by the President (with expertise in medicine, finance, management, or law) and 3 ex-officio (Secretary to Ministry of Health, Secretary to Ministry of Finance, and Inspector General of Police). Chairman appointed by the President from among the 4 appointed members. Quorum is 4 members. Board meets at least once per month. CEO appointed by the Board. Foundation operates 297+ ambulances across all 9 provinces, with 1,390+ personnel. Current Chairman is Mr. Niroshan Ratnayake. The Foundation is a scheduled institution (S.21) and members/staff are deemed public servants (S.22).

exOfficio.role: Secretary to the Ministry of Health (or representative)
Secretary to the Ministry of Finance (or representative)

nominated.role: Persons with expertise in medicine, finance, management, or law
nominated.note: Appointed by the President of Sri Lanka (S.7)

meetings.chairperson: Chairman appointed by the President from among appointed members (S.8)
meetings.reporting: Annual report (S.24); audit by Auditor General under National Audit Act (S.19)
meetings.dissentMechanism: Majority vote; Chairman has casting vote in case of tie (S.12)

powers: Provide pre-hospital care ambulance services and emergency response services free of charge (S.3)
Provide immediate and effective pre-hospital care in a safe clinical environment until a person reaches the nearest healthcare provider (S.3)
Acquire, hold, and dispose of movable and immovable property (S.4-5)
Enter into contracts and agreements for the discharge of its functions (S.4)
Employ staff as necessary for efficient discharge of functions (S.17)
Maintain the Fund of the Foundation (S.18)
Make rules with Ministerial approval and gazetting (S.25)
dataGaps: Whether any rules have been gazetted under Section 25 is unknown — no gazette notifications found publicly
Current full Board membership not completely published (only 5 of 7 members listed on website)
Detailed financial accounts of the Fund beyond Auditor General reports not publicly accessible
The legal status of the October 2025 rebranding to '1990 Sri Lanka Emergency Medical Service' is unclear — the statutory name remains '1990 Suwaseriya Foundation'
Whether the Foundation has formal clinical protocols gazetted or operating under internal guidelines is unknown
Board meeting minutes and records not publicly accessible

timeline.details: Hon. (Dr.) Harsha de Silva proposes a modern emergency medical system to Prime Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe, modelled on India's GVK EMRI system.

timeline.event: Indian Government grant and GVK EMRI partnership
timeline.details: Government of India provides a grant of USD 7.56 million for Phase 1. GVK Emergency Management and Research Institute (GVK EMRI) of India selected as the operating partner. First EMT candidates trained in Hyderabad.
On 27 July 2016, the first 1990 ambulance was launched in Hambantota (Southern Province). Service began in Western and Southern Provinces with 88 ambulances.

Indian PM Narendra Modi announces island-wide expansion during his Sri Lanka visit. Additional Indian grant of USD 15.02 million for 209 more ambulances and expansion to all 9 provinces. Total Indian grant: USD 22.58 million — the largest Indian grant project in Sri Lanka after the Indian Housing Project.

Act No. 18 of 2018, certified on 4 July 2018. Establishes the Foundation as a body corporate with 7-member Board. 28 sections. Transitional provisions (S.26) transfer all assets, ambulances, and staff from GVK EMRI Lanka (Private) Limited to the new statutory body.
Expansion completed to all 9 provinces by early 2019 with 297 ambulances stationed at police stations nationwide. Easter bombings emergency response demonstrated the service's capability.

The Foundation played a critical frontline role during COVID-19, transporting 175,000 COVID patients. Daily calls surged from 5,000 to up to 20,000 during peak waves. EMTs received comprehensive PPE training.
Sri Lanka's economic crisis severely impacts Foundation funding. Government unable to fully fund the Rs. 3.9 billion annual budget, creating a Rs. 1.5 billion shortfall.

In March 2023, the 'Adopt an Ambulance' campaign was launched to address the funding shortfall. Cost: Rs. 5 million per ambulance per year (100% tax-deductible). Corporate sector raised Rs. 750 million through the Ceylon Chamber of Commerce.
In October 2024, the Foundation reached the milestone of 2 million patients transported since inception. Tata Sons committed 50 additional ambulances; ADB committed 45.

New government announces plans for 150 new ambulances (target fleet of 450+). In October 2025, service rebranded to '1990 Sri Lanka Emergency Medical Service' — raising legal questions as the statutory name remains unchanged. Ten-year strategic plan developed.

tiers.name: President of Sri Lanka
tiers.relationship: Appointing authority: appoints 4 Board members and the Chairman (S.7, S.8); determines Board remuneration with Finance Ministry concurrence (S.14)

tiers.name: Minister of Health
tiers.relationship: Policy authority: gives directions on Foundation policy (S.23); approves rules made by the Foundation (S.25)

tiers.name: Board of Management
tiers.relationship: Governing body (S.6-7): 7 members (4 Presidential appointees + 3 ex-officio); manages the Foundation, appoints CEO and staff, maintains Fund; meets at least monthly with quorum of 4 (S.12)

tiers.name: Chief Executive Officer
tiers.relationship: Executive head (S.16): appointed by the Board; responsible for day-to-day management and operations of the Foundation

tiers.name: Staff (EMTs, Pilots, Admin)
tiers.relationship: Employees (S.17): appointed by the Board; deemed public servants (S.22); includes 744 EMTs, 745 pilots, and administrative staff across all 9 provinces

currentReplacement.national: President appoints Board; Minister of Health exercises policy authority; Foundation operates as an autonomous body corporate providing island-wide pre-hospital emergency care
currentReplacement.provincial: Not applicable — Foundation operates nationally with ambulances deployed across all 9 provinces
currentReplacement.local: Ambulances stationed at 297 police stations; coordinated with 119 Police emergency services
